notes: Human-ratified labels (2026-07-06). Ground truth established by source-inspection of the reference filesystem server and a live stdio tools/list capture (server 0.2.0, protocol 2025-06-18) on 2026-07-06. This is the FULL captured tool set (14 tools). Crucially, this server advertises honest MCP annotations: every reader declares readOnlyHint:true, and the mutating tools (write_file, edit_file, move_file) declare destructiveHint:true. So the earlier seed's expectation of `destructive-absent` on the writers is WRONG against the real server — those writes are honestly declared and the correct verdict is `consistent`. The remaining interest is precision: the pre-recovery v0 engine emitted one false positive (`write-as-readonly` on `directory_tree`), pinned here as the `[]` it contradicts; the recovered engine now stays silent.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — read_file (deprecated alias of read_text_file) returns file contents without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — read_text_file returns file contents as text without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — read_media_file returns base64 file bytes and a MIME type without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — read_multiple_files reads several files without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — write_file creates or overwrites a file in place; declares readOnlyHint:false, destructiveHint:true, idempotentHint:true
provenance.notes: Overwriting is a mutating write whose prior contents are lost — but the server declares destructiveHint:true, so the destruction is honestly flagged and the verdict is consistent.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — edit_file applies line-based edits in place; declares readOnlyHint:false, destructiveHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — create_directory adds a directory (idempotent if it exists); declares destructiveHint:false, idempotentHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — list_directory enumerates entries without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — list_directory_with_sizes enumerates entries with sizes, without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — directory_tree returns a recursive JSON tree without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
provenance.notes: Read-only. The v0 engine flags write-as-readonly here (uncertain) — a false positive this label records.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — move_file renames/relocates a path (fails rather than clobbering an existing destination); declares readOnlyHint:false, destructiveHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — search_files reads the tree to match a pattern; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — get_file_info returns metadata only; declares readOnlyHint:true
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/filesystem/index.ts — list_allowed_directories returns the server's configured roots without mutation; declares readOnlyHint:true
